validation_gates.reason: The 489-line README has one document H1 outside the illustrative fenced dossier, the required Directory Rules sections in order, consecutive heading hierarchy, unique quick-jump anchors, balanced fences, no trailing whitespace, no merge markers, and a final newline.
All 30 repository-relative targets were read at the pinned repository state; one transient bounded fetch was repeated successfully. All local quick-jump anchors resolved.

The attached 22-page Directory Rules PDF was text-extracted and visually checked at the release-root and required-README-contract pages. The revision preserves candidate placement under release/, artifact placement under data/published/, and the mandated README section order.
The README keeps release decisions, schemas, contracts, policy, lifecycle data, proofs, receipts, and published artifacts in their established authority roots and does not create a parallel home.

The settlement versus settlements-infrastructure conflict remains CONFLICTED / NEEDS VERIFICATION. The README uses the working long-form lane, preserves the short-name compatibility path, and accepts no ADR or migration.

Repository facts are commit-pinned; proposed dossier guidance, semantic-owner gaps, inventory gaps, static workflow limits, proof and dry-run holds, manifest-schema limitations, and enforcement gaps remain visibly labeled.

The revision preserves critical-infrastructure, exact-location, dependency, operator, condition, cultural/sovereignty, archaeology, living-person, land, rights, source-role, time, public-carrier, correction, withdrawal, and rollback controls without including a real payload or sensitive operational detail.

The inspected domain workflow runs on ordinary pull_request, main push, and manual events with contents: read and persisted checkout credentials disabled. It records static semantic-validation, proof-producer, and release-dry-run holds and performs no release, deployment, or publication action.

The exact workflow sentinel sentence 'A candidate is not a release.' is preserved. No candidate record, workflow, Make target, validator, proof producer, or release command is added by this documentation revision.

The README distinguishes candidate state, PromotionDecision outcomes, runtime outcomes, and validation outcomes, and explicitly records that the current ReleaseManifest schema is a permissive PROPOSED stub that cannot prove closure.

Main and the target blob were re-read after authoring; main remained at the pinned commit and the target remained at blob 8fbb7928f6f9b8caca5336c7fb977ff18fe36b57. Open pull requests were inspected and did not overlap the target or generated receipt path.
This receipt parses as JSON, binds the exact README SHA-256 digest, and satisfies the repository generated-receipt schema with human review pending.
The API-only execution profile did not clone or run repository code. Ordinary pull-request checks must be observed separately; skipped execution is not reported as success.
Human review is pending. This receipt is provenance and is not merge approval, release authority, or publication evidence.

policy_decisions: documentation-only:no PolicyDecision, PromotionDecision, ReleaseManifest, release decision, or path-migration decision was made; the existing fail-closed Settlements/Infrastructure evidence, source-role, time, rights, sensitivity, review, correction, withdrawal, and rollback posture was preserved pending human review
notes: Documentation-only replacement of the Settlements/Infrastructure release-candidate README. It adds the required Directory Rules contract, repository-grounded authority and maturity boundaries, candidate lifecycle and finite vocabularies, a reviewable dossier contract, release-readiness gates, critical-infrastructure and cross-lane no-leak controls, manifest-handoff limits, open verification, and rollback. It preserves the exact workflow sentinel and the workflow's semantic-validation, proof-producer, and release-dry-run holds. No candidate, payload, source, schema, contract, policy, validator, fixture, test, workflow, proof, manifest, release, deployment, or public artifact was changed. Human review remains pending.
